properties.EventDescription: Due to strong winds in the Coastal Otago Region, extra care is required for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles when travelling through the area.

properties.LocationArea: SH 58 Pauatahanui, near Joseph Banks Drive intersection
properties.EventComments: Due to footbridge works, there will be intermittent holds of traffic during school peak times: 8:00am - 9:30am and 2:30pm - 4pm, in both directions with a temporary speed restriction of 30km/h in place. Stop on request and drive with caution.

properties.LocationArea: SH 5 Te Haroto to Te Pohue
properties.EventComments: Due to road resurfacing works, Stop/Go traffic management will be in place during daylight hours, Monday to Saturday, through to late June. Delays of up to 20 minutes are possible. Works have been extended due to weather and ground conditions requiring additional repairs.  A temporary seal will be applied for winter, with crews returning in the warmer months to complete the work. There will be no work over the coming Kings Birthday weekend and will continue on Tuesday 2nd June.
properties.Name: Road Work: SH 5 Te Haroto to Te Pohue

properties.LocationArea: SH 38 Waikaremoana to Tuai
properties.EventComments: Due to under-slip repairs, the road will be closed from Monday 8th June through to mid July 8am to 12pm and then from 1pm to 5pm, Monday to Friday. The road will be open for 1 hour between 12pm and 1pm to allow queued vehicles through. Please note these works are weather dependent and may be postponed at short notice.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 38 Waikaremoana to Tuai

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Opotiki to Matawai (Waioweka Gorge)
properties.EventComments: State Highway 2 Waioweka Gorge is closed from 9pm to 5am Sunday to Thursday from 21 June to 2 July. A convoy will allow traffic through the closure between 1am and 2am. Stop/go traffic management is in place during the day.
properties.AlternativeRoute:
Southbound: Head West on SH2 toward Whakatane, then turn right to stay on SH2, then turn left on to SH30, then turn left on to SH5, then turn left on to Settlers Rd, then continue straight on to Broadlands Rd, then use the on-ramp to join SH1 headed south, then turn left on to SH5, then turn left on to Low Level Rd, then turn right, then turn left on to SH5, then turn on to SH2.

Northbound follow in reverse.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 2 Opotiki to Matawai (Waioweka Gorge)

properties.LocationArea: SH 36 Hauraki Stream Bridge
properties.EventComments: From Wednesday 29 April, traffic will be reduced to one lane about 1km north of the SH36/Hamurana Road intersection, while a culvert is replaced. Shortly afterwards, traffic management will be put in place as part of the Hauraki Stream Bridge replacement. This will include traffic signals, stop/go signage, lane and shoulder closures, and a temporary speed limit of 30km/h. Lane restrictions are expected to remain in place until construction is complete in early 2027.
properties.AlternativeRoute:
Follow the directions of crews on site.
Oversized vehicles intending to use SH36 are required to detour via SH2, SH33, SH30 and SH5 for travel between Rotorua and Tauranga.
properties.Name: Road Work: SH 36 Hauraki Stream Bridge

properties.LocationArea: SH 7 Waipara to Springs Junction

properties.LocationArea: SH 1N Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section
properties.EventComments: Due to the continuation of the road resurfacing, SH1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section, the northbound on and off-ramps at the Victoria Road interchange will be closed 24/7 from 9pm Monday 15 June until mid-July. Please plan ahead and expect delays.
properties.AlternativeRoute:
Northbound SH1 traffic wanting to access Cambridge, needs to exit at the Southern Interchange and use Tirau Road past the Cambridge Golf Club.
Northbound traffic leaving Cambridge can use SH1B and Hamilton Road/Cambridge Road to reconnect with SH1 and the expressway heading north. Northbound traffic leaving Cambridge can also use SH1B/SH26 and back to SH1 at Hamilton
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 1N Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section

Plan your journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel. Please note access to Block Road from SH2 is permanently closed.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 2 Melling, near the intersection with Melling Link

properties.LocationArea: SH 39 Pironghia, at the Mangati Bridge
properties.EventComments: Due to bridge construction works, there is stop/go traffic management with a 30km/h temporary speed restriction in place at the Mangati Bridge. Stop on request and drive with caution.
properties.Name: Road Work: SH 39 Pironghia, at the Mangati Bridge

properties.LocationArea: SH 43 Whangamomona and Taumarunui
properties.EventComments: Following a prior road closure, State Highway 43 is now open with stop go traffic management and temporary speed restrictions in various locations. Road users are advised to take extra care and expect delays up to 30 minutes between Whangamomona and Tangarakau .
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 43 Whangamomona and Taumarunui

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section
properties.EventComments: Due to road resurfacing, SH1 Waikato Expressway, (Cambridge) will be one lane each direction from the Southern Interchange/Tirau Rd to Victoria Rd Interchange at Hautapu from Sunday 1 March. Work then progresses in 5km sections north to Tamahere. Lanes closed 24/7; expect delays at peak times. Allow extra travel time and travel with care through the worksites. Works run until November.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section

properties.LocationArea: SH 88 St Leonards to Roseneath
properties.EventComments: Due to water works, there will be Stop/Go traffic management with a 30km/h speed restriction in place from Monday 15th September to Tuesday 30th June 2026 between 7am and 5pm. Plan your journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 88 St Leonards to Roseneath

properties.LocationArea: SH 35 Te Araroa,  Pohutu Road to Whakaangiangi Rd
properties.EventComments: Following a prior severe weather event, there is temporary traffic management is in place at various locations, including signalised stop/go sites. Please stop on request and take extra care when travelling through the area.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 35 Te Araroa,  Pohutu Road to Whakaangiangi Rd

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Te Kamo Springs Flat RAB
properties.EventComments: A road layout change is in place on SH1 to allow drainage work to begin on the east. A reduced speed limit of 50km/h will remain in place for the duration of construction.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 1 Te Kamo Springs Flat RAB

properties.LocationArea: SH 31 Kawhia
properties.EventComments: Due to a slip on SH31 near Raglan Road, a priority give way is in place 24/7 until further notice. Please take care when passing through the slip site.
properties.Name: Road Hazard: SH 31 Kawhia

properties.LocationArea: SH 29 Belk Road to Takitimu Drive
properties.EventComments:
Due to Road Construction and Tauriko enabling works there will be continuous lane diversions, shoulder closures and Stop/Go operations throughout the period of works. Sunday 8pm to Friday 5pm.
Please drive with care as 30kph, 50kph,60kph and 70kph temporary speed limits will be in place at all times.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 29 Belk Road to Takitimu Drive

properties.LocationArea: SH 80 Pukaki to Mt Cook (Aoraki Mt Cook Highway)
properties.EventComments: Due to an uneven road surface, a temporary speed restriction of 70 km/hr is currently in place. Please take extra care when driving through this area.
properties.Name: Road Work: SH 80 Pukaki to Mt Cook (Aoraki Mt Cook Highway)

properties.LocationArea: Kuaotunu River Bridge Repairs
properties.Content:
FINAL UPDATE THU 17 OCT 2019

Kuaotunu River bridge reopens

Our contractors have done a fantastic job to get the bridge opened ahead of schedule, and more than a week before Labour weekend, despite the heavy rain and strong winds that battered the area earlier this week. Traffic management remains in place, including a 30km/h speed restriction.

Please note that, weather dependent, the bridge will need to close again for the day on Monday (21 Oct) to carry out resurfacing works.

---------------

 

UPDATE TUE 08 OCT 2019

Repairing Kuaotunu bridge to reconnect SH25 

Work to repair the Kuaotunu Bridge on SH25 Coromandel after last month’s storm is progressing well, with the bridge expected to reopen within the next couple of weeks.

The repair team has finished driving the piles on the northern side of the bridge and has switched to the southern side. Once all the piles have been driven they will need to be cut to size and the bridge abutments filled with concrete before work to reinstate the bridge deck can begin.

“We understand that these works are frustrating for local residents and those who travel to the area often, however, our contractors are working as hard as they can to repair the damage as quickly and efficiently as possible,” says Transport Agency Waikato Systems Manager Karen Boyt.  

The Transport Agency has joined together with Thames Coromandel District Council to provide a free shuttle bus service from Kuaotunu to Whitianga every day while the bridge is closed.

The Go Kiwi shuttle departs from outside the Kuaotunu fire station, at 7:30am and 8:30am and then returns from Whitianga at 4:30pm and 5:30pm. From Wednesday 9 October there will also be a lunchtime bus leaving Whitianga at 11.30 and Kuaotunu at 12pm. 

Anyone travelling in the area should check our Journey Planner, www.journeys.nzta.govt.nz or call 0800 44 44 49 before leaving for the most up to date information. 

[image src="/assets/8e66b60fd1/NZTA-3.jpg" id="1023" width="657" height="507" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="NZTA 3"]

[image src="/assets/5b38db22be/nzta-2.jpg" id="1022" width="657" height="586" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="nzta 2"]

 

 ---------------

Following an assessment after recent heavy rain, the SH25 Kuaotunu River bridge has been closed, for around five weeks, to repair weather damage.

We understand the frustration this may cause for those who live in or travel to the area often, however our contractors are working as hard as they can to repair the damage as quickly and efficiently as possible.

During this time detours will be clearly signposted via either SH25A and SH25 or SH25 Thames Coast Road.

 [image src="/assets/dd1a4446a4/Kuaotunu-IMG_STOP.jpg" id="1019" width="251" height="303" class="ss-htmleditorfield-file image right" title="Kuaotunu IMG STOP"]
  • Residents south of Blackjack Road will need to access Kuaotunu from Whitianga.
  • Residents west of the bridge will only have vehicle access from the Coromandel side.
  • Pedestrians will be able to cross the river via a separate, existing walk bridge. 

We hope to have the bridge open again by Labour Weekend, however, this is weather dependant. We will keep the community updated, and thank you for your patience during this time.

 

UPDATE TUE 24 SEP 2019

Repair works to SH25 Kuaotonu River Bridge began on Sunday 22 Sep, following an early morning karakia by local Iwi. Our contractors have started to remove the wooden deck after stripping back the seal layers. The seal and deck have been removed by hand, and efforts made to protect the environment by installing a platform below the deck.

[image src="/assets/b241894b48/Kuaotunu-IMG_6039.jpg" id="1018" width="733" height="550" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="Kuaotunu IMG 6039"]
properties.Name: Road Work: Kuaotunu River Bridge Repairs
